content.problemStatement:
A polygon TTT is said to be inside a polygon SSS if all points that lie strictly inside TTT (not on the perimeter of TTT) also lie strictly inside SSS.

A multiset of polygons {Q1,Q2,…,Qr}\{Q_1, Q_2, \ldots, Q_r\}{Q1​,Q2​,…,Qr​} is said to form a chain if there is a permutation p1,p2,…,prp_1, p_2, \ldots, p_rp1​,p2​,…,pr​ of the integers 111 through rrr such that for each iii (1≤i<r1 \le i \lt r1≤i<r), QpiQ_{p_i}Qpi​​ is inside Qpi+1Q_{p_{i+1}}Qpi+1​​.

You are given NNN convex polygons P1,P2,…,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1​,P2​,…,PN​ in a 2D Cartesian coordinate system. Every 10−10010^{-100}10−100 seconds, you may choose one of the polygons and translate it by upto 10−10010^{-100}10−100 either along the xxx-axis or along the yyy-axis.

Find the minimum amount of time necessary to make all NNN polygons form a chain or decide that it is impossible.
constraints: the sum of MMM over all polygons does not exceed 100100100
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (10 points):** N=2N = 2N=2 and both polygons are axis-aligned rectangles
**Subtask #3 (20 points):** all polygons are axis-aligned rectangles

`Explanation**Example case 1:** Both P1P_1P1​ and P2P_2P2​ are squares. If we move P2P_2P2​ by 111 unit in the xxx-direction and by 222 units in the yyy-direction, the vertices of P1P_1P1​ become the midpoints of edges of P2P_2P2​.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer NNN. The descriptions of NNN polygons follow.
For each polygon:

The first line contains a single integer MMM denoting the number of its vertices.
The following MMM lines describe the vertices in counterclockwise order. Each of these lines contains two space-separated integers xxx and yyy denoting the coordinates of one vertex.
content.outputFormat:
If it is impossible to make the polygons form a chain, print a single line containing the integer −1-1−1.

Otherwise, print a single line containing one real number ― the minimum amount of time. Your answer will be considered correct if its absolute or relative error does not exceed 10−610^{-6}10−6.
